t_ch: 可惜，他们无法参透这秘密，只能对我施加自己所能想到的最恶毒的刑罚。
t_en: Unfortunately, they failed to uncover the secret, so they resorted to the most brutal punishments they could imagine.

t_ch: 有些人追求力量是为毁灭他的仇敌，有些人追求力量却是为了变成他的仇敌…椒丘，你是哪一种？
t_en: Some seek power to destroy their enemies, while others seek power to become their enemies... Which one are you, Jiaoqiu?

t_ch: 啊，我明白了！曜青的医士，你是最可悲的那一种人——你追求力量，是为了与别人分享它，用它来行善。
t_en: Ah, I see! You're the most pitiful of them all, healer of the Yaoqing! You seek power to share it with others and do good.

t_ch: 那就让我来告诉你，你想知道的答案。
t_en: Well, then let me tell you the answer you seek.

t_ch: 在古老的传说里，步离人的始祖都蓝不满于有限的生命和力量，他渴望主宰天空，成为群星的主人。
t_en: In our ancient legends, Duran, the ancestor of the borisin, was dissatisfied with his limited lifespan and power. He yearned to dominate the skies and become the master of all the stars.

t_ch: 为此他牺牲了无数步离人与狐人的生命，注入长生主恩赐的泉水，在基因巫术的催动下，水中孕育了一个奇迹——「胎动之月」。
t_en: To achieve his goal, he sacrificed the lives of countless borisin and foxians, pouring them into the spring bestowed by the Master of Immortality. Through a form of genetic sorcery, the water gave birth to a miracle — the Lunar Embryo.

t_ch: 攀上「月亮」的产床后，都蓝从中获得了自己梦寐以求的东西，一轮形如赤红满月的「心脏」。都蓝切开自己的胸膛，用这轮赤月替换了自己的心。
t_en: Duran ascended {RUBY_B#Moon}the birthing bed{RUBY_E#} and obtained what he desired: A heart in the shape of a deep-red full moon. He cut open his chest and replaced his original heart with this crimson moon.

t_ch: 真是有步离色彩的神话…
t_en: How typical. Just another borisin myth...

t_ch: 你尽可以嘲笑它野蛮疯狂。但只有我知道，它真实不虚。
t_en: Laugh all you want, but only I know the truth behind every word.

t_ch: 别拿神话来糊弄我！
t_en: I'm not buying your absurd myth!

t_ch: 建木也是荒诞不经的神话。但你们仙舟人明白，它真实不虚。
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor is also an absurd myth, but you Xianzhou folk know it's true.

t_ch: 这颗心月世世代代跳动在步离战首们的身体里。因为步离人决出战首的仪式，正是由继任之人剖开前任战首的胸膛，吃下这神肉，让强者拥有它！
t_en: This moon heart beats within the body of every generation of the Borisin Warhead. In the ritual that decides the new Warhead, the successor cuts open the previous Warhead's chest and consumes the divine heart, thus possessing it as their own!

t_ch: 「吞噬」，这是生命得以延续、茁壮的真谛。它凝聚着被我们吞噬的猎物的生命精华，也让我们变得越发强壮！
t_en: Devouring is the true essence of life, allowing us to endure and thrive. It gathers the life essence of all the prey we consume, making us stronger and stronger!

t_ch: 受刑七百年，我曾以为一切毫无希望了，但如今，这轮心脏再度跳动起来了。
t_en: I thought all hope was lost after seven centuries of torment, but now, this heart is beating once again.

t_ch: 好了，轮到你告诉我…这位「天击将军」的一切了。
t_en: Alright, now it's your turn to tell me... everything about the Merlin's Claw.

t_ch: 椒丘…我找到你了。
t_en: Jiaoqiu... I've found you.

t_ch: 演武仪典召开前两个时辰\n\n迴星港
t_en: Two dual-hours before the Wardance convenes\n\nStargazer Navalia

t_ch: 迴星港
t_en: Stargazer Navalia

t_ch: 将军，虽然我不该质疑您的指示。但如果我没听错，你说你已经获得了关于步离人的一手情报？
t_en: I know I'm in no position to question your orders, but if I understand correctly, you already have intelligence about the borisin?

t_ch: 不错。我的部下正在时刻寻找线索。貊泽，目前情况如何。
t_en: That's right. My people are busy collecting clues now. What's the situation, Moze?

t_ch: 将军，在椒丘彻底失联前，我捕捉了他身上的玉兆信号，要不了多久应该能查到他们的落脚处。
t_en: General, before we completely lost contact with Jiaoqiu, I picked up a signal from his jade abacus. It won't take long before we locate their whereabouts.

t_ch: 既然这样，咱们难道不该立刻与他汇合，和他一同铲除步离人。怎么又跑到迴星港来了？
t_en: If that's the case, shouldn't we immediately go there and join forces to eliminate the borisin? Why are we returning to Stargazer Navalia?

t_ch: 看来你家将军只教了你剑术，却没教你如何「巡猎」。
t_en: Looks like your general has only taught you swordplay, not the art of The Hunt.

t_ch: 步离人有句古谚「在森林里，猎人与猎物的身份随时改变」。眼下我们自居猎人，但盲目追着他们跑，到头来我们反倒会成为呼雷的猎物。
t_en: There's an old saying among the borisin: "In the forest, the hunter may easily become the hunted." While we may call ourselves the hunters now, chasing them blindly will only make us become the prey.

t_ch: 这家伙…能怎么狩猎我们？
t_en: How can Hoolay... possibly hunt us down?

t_ch: 呼雷人数可不占优啊？
t_en: But we have the advantage in numbers, right?

t_ch: 将军莫非是在和彦卿说笑？一头被关押了七百年的恶狼，就算有党羽协助，相较于罗浮云骑的数量也微不足道，怎么可能把我们当成猎物？
t_en: Are you joking, General? He's just a wolf who's been imprisoned for seven centuries. Even with his accomplices, they're no match for us numerous Luofu Cloud Knights. How could he consider us his prey?

t_ch: 两位，收起轻敌之心。呼雷绝不是能被你们轻易斩杀的步离人。人数多寡对这怪物而言毫无意义。
t_en: Do not underestimate the enemy. Hoolay is not an ordinary borisin that can be easily killed. The advantage in numbers means nothing when we face that monster.

t_ch: 七百年未曾噬人的恶兽，在演武仪典召开前潜入了罗浮街巷，这才是如今的局势最凶险难测的地方。
t_en: A vicious beast, who hasn't tasted blood and flesh in seven centuries, is now lurking on the streets of the Luofu right before the Wardance. This is what makes the situation treacherous and unpredictable.

t_ch: 寻常百姓的人命，就像随时可能被这家伙一口吞下的肉……
t_en: To him, the lives of ordinary people are just meat, ready to be devoured at any moment...

t_ch: 没错，为此，我们必须一击得手，不然，局势将会变得不可收拾。
t_en: Exactly. That's why we must succeed in our first strike, or the situation will spiral out of control.

t_ch: 你们应该听说过兵法中「围三阙一，网开一面」的战术吧？任猎物疯狂逃窜可不行。好猎人要做足准备，在最合适的位置等待猎物上门。
t_en: You've heard of this military tactic, right? When you surround your enemy, leave one side open for them to escape. A skilled hunter must be well-prepared, waiting for the prey at the most advantageous position.

t_ch: 步离人想去哪儿呢？
t_en: Where do those borisin want to go?

t_ch: 迴星港就是他们最可能来的地方？
t_en: Is Stargazer Navalia the most likely place for them to come to?

t_ch: 想象一下，咱们是刚逃离幽囚狱的犯人。尽管能伪装变化，混入人群，但我并不打算在此久留，眼下最需要的是什么？
t_en: What would we do if we were the escapees from The Shackling Prison? While we can disguise ourselves and blend in with the crowd, we don't plan on staying here for long. In this case, what do we need most now?

t_ch: …载我们逃离罗浮的星槎。我在迴星港发现的步离人正是在为他们准备逃跑的船只。
t_en: ...A starskiff to get us out of the Luofu. The borisin we discovered at Stargazer Navalia were actually preparing a vessel for their escape.

t_ch: 猜测猎物可能的去向，接着就该慢慢收网了。
t_en: We predict their next move, and we take them down.

t_ch: 来吧，从你发现步离人踪迹的地方开始，我们让猎物无处可逃。
t_en: Let's start from where you found those borisin and corner our prey.

t_ch: 上次我们跟踪步离人的位置就在这里，看上去跟平时一样。
t_en: This is where we ran into the borisin last time. It looks the same as usual.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>在那之后，我立即命人封锁了消息，并尽量使一切维持原状。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Because I blocked all the relevant news, to make it seem as if nothing had happened.</color>

t_ch: 步离人会不会察觉到了？
t_en: Will the borisin notice something?

t_ch: 准备星槎的同伙没有回音，这应该会让他们有所警惕。
t_en: Losing contact with their accomplices preparing the starskiff will surely put them on guard.

t_ch: 彦卿会不会打草惊蛇了？
t_en: Yanqing may have alerted the borisin?

t_ch: 倘若彦卿没有「打草惊蛇」，眼前恐怕还毫无头绪呢。
t_en: If Yanqing hadn't scared them off, we would not have many clues.

t_ch: 不过，越是准备周详的计划，一旦被打乱，作出反应的时间也就越长。步离人中如果还有想赌一把，坚持原计划出逃的，一定会回到这里确认迴星港的情况。
t_en: However, the more well-prepared a plan is, the longer it takes to adjust when it's disrupted. If any borisin wants to stick to the original plan despite the risks, they'll surely come back to Stargazer Navalia to check the situation.

t_ch: 你是说迴星港里此刻就有步离人？
t_en: Are you suggesting that there are borisin here at Stargazer Navalia, right now?

t_ch: 你难道没有发觉，这片自动运行的区域里，今日的人有些多的不太寻常？
t_en: This is an automated area, so don't you think that there are a bit too many people here today?

t_ch: 首先是那两个天舶司职员。打从我们来到这里，他们就在悄悄观察我们。
t_en: First, look at those two Sky-Faring Commission staff members. They've been observing us in secret since we arrived here.

t_ch: 接下来是那边的云骑士兵，如果是来巡逻的，那就是没有遵守两人一组的执勤规制。
t_en: Then, there's a Cloud Knight soldier over there. Perhaps he's here for patrol, but each patrol team must consist of two members, and obviously, he's not following the rules.

t_ch: 最后是那个穿着工造司服色的匠人，煞有介事地对着装置看个不停。
t_en: Lastly, there's that craftsman wearing Artisanship Commission clothing. He's unusually focused on checking that device.

t_ch: 想好了目标的话，咱们就去探探底。
t_en: If you have a target in mind, let's go investigate.

t_ch: 你们是什么人，不知道迴星港已经被封锁了吗？
t_en: Who are you? Don't you know that Stargazer Navalia is on lockdown now?

t_ch: 唉，几个不请误入的游客嘛，咱们好好说话。
t_en: Hey, chill out. They're just a few lost tourists.

t_ch: 迴星港被封锁了？
t_en: Stargazer Navalia is on lockdown now?

t_ch: …迴星港是罗浮仙舟的造船港，如果天舶司要封锁港口，肯定是有公文通知吧？
t_en: ...But Stargazer Navalia is the shipbuilding port of the Xianzhou Luofu. If the Sky-Faring Commission called for a lockdown, there should be official documents proving it, right?

t_ch: 公文？当然有了。云骑军得知有人潜入迴星港。
t_en: Official documents? Of course, there are. The Cloud Knights are aware that some people have infiltrated this port.

t_ch: …哦，合情合理，所以你们现在是排查步离人？
t_en: ...That makes perfect sense. So, you're now investigating the borisin?

t_ch: 我们收到了上级指示，为了避免无关人等干扰云骑检查，我们特地将这里封锁了起来，请你们理解我们的工作。
t_en: Yeah, we've received orders from the higher-ups, so we've blocked off this area to prevent any interference with our inspection. Thank you for your understanding.

t_ch: 我们收到了上级指示，为了避免无关人等干扰云骑检查，我们特地将这里封锁了起来，请你们理解我们的工作。
t_en: Yeah, we've received orders from the higher-ups, so we've blocked off this area to prevent any interference with our inspection. Thank you for your understanding.

t_ch: 唉…啊！彦卿骁卫！还有飞霄将军？
t_en: Ahh... Ah! Lieutenant Yanqing! And General Feixiao?

t_ch: 云骑大哥，你知道步离人越狱的事情了吧？
t_en: You're aware of the borisin prison break, right?

t_ch: 接到消息了，我会时刻保持警惕！
t_en: Yeah, I've received the news, and I'll be on the alert!

t_ch: 根据我的观察，你一直在盯着竞锋舰的方向，所以你发现了什么状况吗？
t_en: Looks like you've been keeping an eye on the Skysplitter. Have you noticed anything unusual?

t_ch: 竞锋舰一切如常，没有特别的状况！
t_en: No, everything is normal on the ship. Nothing out of the ordinary!

t_ch: （质疑他所处的位置）
t_en: (Question his location)

t_ch: 这地方可不是云骑执勤的位置吧？
t_en: This is not a location for a Cloud Knight on guard duty, is it?

t_ch: 哦，这个地方视野不错，你是来这里观察竞锋舰的吧？
t_en: Well, this spot offers a great view. Are you here to keep an eye on the Skysplitter?
Yes...

t_ch: （询问竞锋舰的情况）
t_en: (Ask about the Skysplitter)

t_ch: 你看了这么久的竞锋舰，发现了什么吗？
t_en: You've been observing the Skysplitter for a while. Have you noticed anything?

t_ch: …只是在这里看着舰船的飞行轨迹确实没什么意思，要是能上船，我最想看的还是彦卿大人的守擂比赛……
t_en: ...Honestly, just watching the ship from here isn't too thrilling. I wish I could go on board and witness Lieutenant Yanqing's contests...

t_ch: 说到这个，彦卿大人，您不是应该在竞锋舰上吗？现在不回到竞锋舰上没关系吗？
t_en: Speaking of which, Lieutenant Yanqing, aren't you supposed to be on the Skysplitter? Is it alright for you to be here instead?

t_ch: 谢谢你的支持，我有更重要的事情要去做。
t_en: Thank you for your concern, but I have more pressing matters to attend to.
Ah...

t_ch: （结束问话）
t_en: (End the questioning)

t_ch: 士兵，你有任务在身，给我集中精神！
t_en: You're on duty, soldier. Stay focused!

t_ch: 遵命！
t_en: Yes, ma'am!

t_ch: 喂，你们三位，如果不是官方人员，请不要在迴星港里随便转悠。啊…是彦卿骁卫。
t_en: Hey, you there! Please refrain from wandering around Stargazer Navalia if you're not... Oh, it's you, Lieutenant Yanqing.

t_ch: 难道迴星港被封锁了？
t_en: Is Stargazer Navalia on lockdown now?

t_ch: 封锁倒也没封锁…只是我在这儿排查一个古怪的问题。
t_en: No, not at all... Actually, I'm here to address a peculiar issue.

t_ch: 你们不也在这儿乱逛吗？
t_en: Aren't you wandering around here as well?

t_ch: 我是官方人员，被派来专门来排查一个古怪的问题。
t_en: I'm an official staff member assigned to investigate a peculiar issue.

t_ch: 古怪的问题？
t_en: A peculiar issue?

t_ch: 没错，有计划外的星槎被制造了出来，全都从迴星港的这个码头里产出。是流水线的产能规划又出错了吗？
t_en: Yeah, we've had some unscheduled starskiffs being constructed at this dock in Stargazer Navalia. Could there have been an error in the production planning for the assembly line?

t_ch: 这可太奇怪了，演武仪典之前生产的星槎都应该做好规划了啊。
t_en: This is just weird. I thought all the pending starskiffs from before the Wardance had been scheduled already.

t_ch: 实在不好意思，我必须先专注眼前的工作了。有问题稍后再谈。
t_en: I'm sorry, but I need to focus on my work right now. If you have any questions, we can discuss them later.

t_ch: 实在不好意思，我必须先专注眼前的工作了。有问题稍后再谈。
t_en: I'm sorry, but I need to focus on my work right now. If you have any questions, we can discuss them later.

t_ch: 有一伙步离人假扮身份重新回到了迴星港，答案已经显而易见了——
t_en: So, it seems that some borisin have returned to Stargazer Navalia in disguise. The answer seems quite obvious...

t_ch: 应该是天舶司的职员。
t_en: The staff members from the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 我也这么认为。
t_en: That's my suspicion as well.

t_ch: 应该是可疑的云骑士兵。
t_en: The Cloud Knight soldier.

t_ch: 那个士兵应该只是有些走神，我认为最可疑的应该是天舶司职员。
t_en: I believe he was just slacking off. In my opinion, the staff members from the Sky-Faring Commission appear more suspicious.

t_ch: 应该是工造司匠人。
t_en: The craftsman from the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 我看这个人反而是为排查星槎而来的。最可疑的应该是天舶司职员。
t_en: I think he's genuinely here to investigate the starskiffs, as he claimed. In my opinion, the staff members from the Sky-Faring Commission are the most suspicious.

t_ch: 你还记得我说过云骑军封锁了消息，并尽量使一切维持原状吗？这意味着，他们不应该封锁迴星港。
t_en: Remember when I mentioned that the Cloud Knights blocked all relevant news, to make it seem as if nothing happened? Stargazer Navalia is not under lockdown.

t_ch: 而那两位职员却以「迴星港封锁」为理由劝我们离开，这与云骑军原定的计划相悖。
t_en: But on the contrary, those staff members used "Stargazer Navalia is on lockdown" as an excuse to persuade us to leave. This contradicts the Cloud Knights' plans.

t_ch: 拙劣的伪装到此为止了，就算他们想要隐瞒也不可能逃脱。
t_en: Their flimsy disguise has been exposed. Even if they try to hide, there's no way they can escape us.

t_ch: 你们几位怎么还在这儿？我刚才说了，迴星港已经被封锁了。
t_en: Why are you still here? As I said, Stargazer Navalia is on lockdown now.

t_ch: 我可从未下过这样的命令。
t_en: No, I never issued such an order.

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 你们也许从未在战场上见过我，但一定听过我的名字。不过是谁准许你用问题来回答问题了！告诉我——
t_en: Perhaps you haven't met me on the battlefield, but I'm certain you've heard my name. Now, I'm asking you a question, so answer me! Tell me...

t_ch: 你们还有多少同伙？原定计划打算在何处接应呼雷？
t_en: How many more of your comrades are here? Where were you planning to meet Hoolay?

t_ch: 那个女人…是曜青的将军！你在等什么，动手！
t_en: This woman... She's the general of the Yaoqing! What are you still waiting for!? Attack her!

t_ch: 为什么不杀了我？
t_en: Why don't you kill me?

t_ch: 我还有些问题没有得到回答。
t_en: Because I need answers.

t_ch: 曾经身为战奴的你，应该很熟悉我们这些都蓝子裔……
t_en: You were our war slave, so you should know us descendants of Duran...

t_ch: 尽管试试吧，尽管用你知道的那些手段逼我开口。我能回答你的…永远只有鲜血和爪牙。
t_en: You want me to talk? Fine, try whatever you want, but I assure you, you won't get anything from me... only fangs and blood.

t_ch: 如果那样做，我和你们也就别无二致了。
t_en: If I were to do that, I wouldn't be any different from you.

t_ch: 我是刺入狼心的锋镝，我赐你速死。
t_en: I am the arrow that pierces the wolf's heart. I grant you a swift death.

t_ch: …这正合我意。离群的狼，早就做好了无法返回故乡的准备。
t_en: ...That's exactly what I desire. Wolves that leave the pack are prepared for the fate of never returning home.

t_ch: 可惜，你没能抓住我的伙伴…他会向伙伴们示警——
t_en: But sadly, you didn't catch my partner... He will alert them...

t_ch: 在你阖眼之前，你应该知道，是我允许他逃走。你的伙伴会带着云骑找到呼雷的下落。
t_en: Before you close your eyes, you should know that I allowed him to escape, because he will lead the Cloud Knights to Hoolay's hideout.

t_ch: 啊啊啊——
t_en: Arrrghhh...

t_ch: 这就是「网开一面」么…？放任步离人逃走，然后顺着他的路线追猎。
t_en: Is this how you... "leave one side open"...? You let that borisin escape so you could track him.

t_ch: 这是步离人最常用的狩猎战术。
t_en: This is the most common hunting tactic used by borisin.

t_ch: 我太了解这些孽物的手段了。他们总会留下逃生的道路，任由猎物仓惶离开。随后便是近乎戏耍般的逐杀追猎，在牺牲者们的惨状中，步离人总会获得莫大的满足……
t_en: I know the methods of these abominations all too well. They always leave an escape route for their prey, allowing them to flee in fear. Then they chase them like it's a game. Borisin get great satisfaction from the last struggles of their victims...

t_ch: 这些都是您亲眼见证的事情吗？那个家伙把你称为…「战奴」，所以您到底经历了什么啊？
t_en: Have you witnessed these things firsthand? That guy called you a... war slave... What exactly have you experienced?

t_ch: 一段模糊的，几乎快记不清的过去罢了。在许久以前，我…曾是行走在他们中的一员。
t_en: Well, just a hazy past that I can barely recollect. Long ago... I was one of them.

t_ch: 你是步离人？！
t_en: Wait, you're a borisin!?

t_ch: 步离人这三个字，只能用来称呼那些狼头恩主。我是一个出生在「沦陷地」的狐人……
t_en: The word "borisin" was only used to refer to those wolf masters. I'm a foxian who was born in one of the "fallen territories"...

t_ch: 在狼群眼中，我们只是流淌着狐血的耗材，是战场冲锋时用来拖住仙舟攻势的炮灰。
t_en: In their eyes, we're mere consumables, cannon fodder used to slow down Xianzhou attacks on the battlefield.

t_ch: 可你是个狐人？
t_en: But you're a foxian, right?

t_ch: 不是所有狐人都能有幸出生在仙舟疆域中的…我成长在步离人所放牧的世界。曜青称那些地方为「沦陷地」。
t_en: Not all foxians are lucky enough to be born on a Xianzhou ship... I grew up in one of the worlds where borisin roam, known as the "fallen territories" by the people of the Yaoqing.

t_ch: 对狼头恩主们而言，狐人只是他们的财产…是战场冲锋时用来拖住仙舟攻势的炮灰。
t_en: To the wolf masters, foxians are nothing but property, cannon fodder used to slow down the Xianzhou attacks on the battlefield.

t_ch: 但将军…活下来了，不但如此，还成为了曜青的将军！如果有机会，我很想听听将军过去的故事。
t_en: But you... survived. And not only that, you became the general of the Yaoqing! If I get the chance, I'd love to hear stories of your past, General.

t_ch: 说到这个，你可提醒我了，眼下不是讲故事的时候。貊泽，迴星港这边已经放出了「警告」，你这边情况如何？
t_en: Speaking of stories, you've reminded me that now is not the time for them. Moze, we've sent the "warning" from Stargazer Navalia. What's the situation on your end?

t_ch: 我找到了他们的位置，椒丘正在与呼雷周旋，他…示意我不要露面。
t_en: I've found them. Jiaoqiu is trying to stall Hoolay, and he signaled me... to not reveal myself.

t_ch: 相信椒丘的判断，继续监视，我们马上就来。
t_en: Trust his judgment, and keep watching. We'll be right there.

t_ch: 放走诱饵，迴星港的示警一定会断去呼雷袭击此处的可能。接着他又该如何应对呢？
t_en: I believe the "warning" we sent will stop Hoolay from attacking Stargazer Navalia. What will he do next?

t_ch: 狩猎还没结束。椒丘，你可一定要平安无事啊……
t_en: The hunt is not over yet. Stay safe, Jiaoqiu...

t_ch: 演武仪典召开前一个时辰\n\n长乐天
t_en: One dual-hour before the Wardance convenes\n\nExalting Sanctum

t_ch: 长乐天
t_en: Exalting Sanctum

t_ch: 求求你…救…我……
t_en: Please... save... me...

t_ch: 我还想活下去…大夫……
t_en: I want to live... Doctor...

t_ch: 椒丘，快找个医用机巧给那个孩子施针！快！
t_en: Jiaoqiu! Find a medical ingenium and give that kid a shot! Hurry!

t_ch: 我…我明白了，前线怎么样了？
t_en: I... I see. How is the front line?

t_ch: 步离人的兽舰已经在方壶上登陆了…要不了多久，这儿也会被攻破。
t_en: The borisin beast ships have already landed on the Fanghu... It won't be long before this place is overrun as well.

t_ch: 月御将军呢…她有消息吗？我是她的医士，这时候我应该留在她的身边。
t_en: What about General Yueyu... Any news from her? I'm her healer. I should stay by her side at a time like this.

t_ch: 将军让我转告你，她不回来了，她必须守住「瞰云镜」。
t_en: She asked me to tell you that she's not coming back. She must protect the Cloudpeer Telescope.

t_ch: 将军让你…救救那个孩子，她像疯了一样战斗，只为了把我们所有人都带回这里！
t_en: General asks you to... to save that kid. The girl fought desperately just to bring us all back here alive!

t_ch: 我从没见过那样可怕的战斗方式…她的身体…我感觉她整个人似乎都撑开了…就像……
t_en: I've never seen such a brutal fighting style... Her body... it's like she was split open... just like...

t_ch: 伤者的血压快见底了。听见了没有，椒丘！
t_en: Her blood pressure is dropping. Do you hear me, Jiaoqiu?

t_ch: 我听得很清楚…准备一支「颠踬散」。我们必须现在开始手术！
t_en: Loud and clear... Get me some Tumbledust! We've got to start the operation right now!

t_ch: 我…一定会把她带回来的！
t_en: I... I will bring her back!

t_ch: …这就是你一心想从我身上破解秘密的原因？你能听见我说话吗，椒丘？
t_en: ...Is that why you're so determined to learn my secrets? Do you hear me, Jiaoqiu?

t_ch: 我听见了，我听得很清楚。
t_en: I do. Loud and clear.

t_ch: 在三十年前方壶仙舟的那场大战中，她拯救了你们所有人，但却在濒死的时刻意外察觉到了体内流淌着步离血脉。
t_en: So, she saved you all in that great battle on the Xianzhou Fanghu three decades ago, but to your surprise, you discovered her borisin bloodline while she was at death's door.

t_ch: 末度告诉我，她是从蚀月猎群里逃离的战奴。何等奇妙的因果，她竟和我同出于一个部落。
t_en: Mok Tok told me that she was a war slave who escaped from the Eclipse Pack. What a twist of fate. Turns out she's from the same clan as I am.

t_ch: 原来如此，难怪那个狐人战斗时所展现的力量，果断和残忍…如此惊人！那全是拜她的步离血脉所赐。杂种…可憎的杂种将军。
t_en: Now I understand. No wonder she displayed... such astounding power, determination, and cruelty in battle...! Turns out it's all because of her borisin bloodline. Mutt... despicable mutt.

t_ch: 而她用自己血脉中的馈赠摧毁了步离人。
t_en: And she used the gift of her bloodline to destroy the borisin.

t_ch: 「月狂」，狼之赐福，狐之诅咒。对于步离人来说，在战斗中被月狂撕裂身体，兽化变形…是为无上喜乐。但对你们这些自愈力有限的狐人来说，它是死路一条。
t_en: Moon Rage, a blessing for wolves, and a curse for foxes. For borisin, to have their bodies torn apart by Moon Rage and transform into a beast... is the ultimate joy, but for you foxians with poor regenerative abilities, it means certain death.

t_ch: 伴随涌上心头的燃烧怒血，这位狐人将军敌我不分，鏖战不休，身躯上绽开的伤痕不是来自敌人的武器，而是她不能承受的巨大力量。终有一日，她将作为怪物，四分五裂地死去。
t_en: With the burning fury in her heart, that foxian general will eventually lose her sanity and indulge in endless bloodshed. The scars on her body will not be caused by enemy weapons, but by the immense power she can't withstand. One day, she will be torn apart and die as a monster.

t_ch: 而为了回报她的救命之恩，你打算倾尽所能，去挑战这个无法解开的谜题。
t_en: And in return for her saving your life, you intend to give everything to solve this impossible puzzle.

t_ch: 呼雷…你知道身为医士，最可悲的事情是什么吗？
t_en: Hoolay... do you know the saddest thing about being a healer?

t_ch: 一直以来，我费尽毕生所学，想从你这样的怪物手中夺回那些赴死的生命。我精疲力尽地瘫坐下来、双手颤抖，我在想我所做的一切都是有意义的……
t_en: All this time, I've devoted my life to bringing back those who sacrificed their lives to monsters like you. I exhausted myself, and my hands trembled, but I believed everything I did was meaningful...

t_ch: 可他们又再一次奔向战场。于是，我听到了他们的死讯。他们死在你们的爪牙之下，在星槎坠毁的火焰里，在帝弓的光矢中……
t_en: But once again, they rushed into battle, and then I heard of their deaths. They died under your claws, in your jaws, amidst the flames of crashing starskiffs, and under the Lux Arrow of Reignbow...

t_ch: 我像是个徒劳的白痴，从名为死亡的鼎镬中，捞起一尾名叫生命的鱼——看着它一个挣扎，又再次跳进了滚沸的汤汁里。
t_en: Like a useless idiot, I saved a fish named "Life" out of the cauldron called "Death," only to watch it struggle and dive back into the boiling broth.

t_ch: 我问自己，是什么让他们在伤愈后，又不顾一切地奔赴死地？为什么不珍惜得来不易的生命？这让我感到迷茫、空虚……
t_en: So I asked myself, why were they so eager to run toward their death after they had recovered from their wounds? Why wouldn't they value their hard-won life? All the doubts left me feeling lost...

t_ch: 呵呵…我在你身上嗅到了深入骨髓的绝望。
t_en: Heh... I can smell your desperation, all the way down to your bone marrow.

t_ch: 最后，我明白了，离去亦有「价值」。他们将离去的重量，压在了我们这些活人的心上，给了我们更多的力量。他们用死亡这枚硬币，换回了更多……
t_en: Eventually, I realized that their deaths held value. They placed the weight of their sacrifice on the living, granting us strength. With a coin forged by their deaths, they exchanged something more in return...

t_ch: 这就是如今我所做的一切，紧紧跟在你的身边，只为用我的双眼见证一个结果：你的死亡……
t_en: Everything I'm doing now, following you so closely, is for just one reason: Witnessing your death with my own two eyes...

t_ch: 你的死亡，亦有价值。它将换来演武仪典的平安；还有被我治愈的飞霄。
t_en: Even your death has value. It will pave the way for a peaceful Wardance and a fully cured Feixiao.

t_ch: 哼，你可悲的脑袋里转动的念头，真是不出所料。
t_en: Hmph, the thoughts in your pathetic head are hardly surprising.

t_ch: 你早就知道？
t_en: Did you already know this?

t_ch: 不错，因为没有谁比步离人更清楚「死亡」所能带来的价值。而像你这样见惯死亡的医士，简单的恐惧也吓阻不了你。
t_en: Yes, I did. As borisin, we understand the value of "death" more than anyone. And as a healer who has witnessed so much death, you won't be swayed by fear.

t_ch: 真遗憾。在听完这个故事的刹那，我心中竟然升起了一丝对你的敬意。
t_en: What a shame. What a shame. Your story actually sparked a trace of respect for you in my heart.

t_ch: 你那颗流淌着毒血的心脏里…也会升起「尊重」这种情感吗？
t_en: Can you even feel "respect"... with your corrupted heart?

t_ch: 当然，因为我恍惚嗅到了同类的味道。可惜，你终究只是一条软弱无力的狐狸。
t_en: Of course, because I caught a whiff of my own kind in you. Unfortunately, in the end, you're still just a weak fox.

t_ch: 如同「狼之古训」所说：赐狼以穷途，令其寻得前路。饲狼以死数，令其食能果腹。苟且偷安，无处得生；抵死鏖杀，万世长存。
t_en: As the Wolf's Creed goes: Gift the wolf a dead end, where new paths arise. Raise him to a doomed fate, where satiation lies. The cowards, in forgotten corners, meet their unworthy demise. Yet the valorous, in brutal battles, embrace their eternal prize...

t_ch: 这也是我暂时留着你的原因，我会向你展示步离人对仇敌的最大敬意：
t_en: That's why I'm keeping you alive for now. I want to show you how borisin truly respect their enemies.

t_ch: 吞噬你们的血肉，滋养我们的筋骨，粉碎你们的愿景，开辟我们的猎途；要让你们的死魂灵好好见证，未来属于我！
t_en: We will consume your flesh and blood, nourishing our own. We will crush your hopes and dreams, clearing a path for our hunt! Your feeble souls will witness a new future, a future that belongs to me!

t_ch: 大人！负责接应的同胞发来了消息，迴星港的布置被人发现，我们现在必须立刻动身了！
t_en: My Lord! We've received a message. Our arrangement at Stargazer Navalia has been discovered. We must act quickly!

t_ch: 这一切也并没有出乎我的意料。
t_en: That doesn't surprise me at all.

t_ch: 末度…这是什么声音？
t_en: Mok Tok... What's that noise?

t_ch: 是竞锋舰。举行演武仪典的星船要起航了。该死，到时候天舶司会清空航路！我们的星槎要是想逃走，一定会被发现的。
t_en: It's the Skysplitter. The ship that the Wardance will be held on is about to set off! The Sky-Faring Commission will clear the air routes! And if our starskiffs try to escape, we'll be spotted.

t_ch: 冷静，末度！看看你现在的样子，东躲西藏，提心吊胆…哪里还有半点步离人的尊严！
t_en: Calm down, Mok Tok! Look at you now, hiding and fearful... Where is your borisin dignity?

t_ch: 我说过，为了能让您顺利回归，我不需要什么尊严！只要您能返回步离人的猎群，一切就还有希望！
t_en: As I said, I'd sacrifice my dignity for your return! As long as you can come back to the borisin packs, there's still hope!

t_ch: 希望？步离人早就遗忘了「狼之古训」！软弱的野兽将希望寄托于强者庇护，强大的野兽则会自己挣杀出一条血路！
t_en: Hope? The borisin have forgotten the Wolf's Creed! Weak creatures put their hope in the strong, but the strong fight their way out!

t_ch: 如今的你们只想要迎回「呼雷」，而不是决出新主，已经证明了整个族群的没落。而那个操纵你们前来营救我的先知，不过是个试图利用都蓝血裔的骗子！
t_en: Bringing me back instead of choosing a new master only proves the decline of our pack. And as for the prophet who manipulated you into saving me, she's just a liar trying to use Duran's offspring!

t_ch: 末度，让我来告诉你步离人如何崛起！我们绝不会像老鼠一样躲藏在仙舟的街巷间，你应当是露出獠牙的饿狼，行走在满是羔羊的牧群。
t_en: Mok Tok, let me tell you how the borisin will rise to power! We won't hide like rats in the streets of the Xianzhou! We will be ravenous wolves, walking amidst a herd of lambs with our fangs bared.

t_ch: 战首大人，我们的狼群不在这里！我们不能贸然开战！
t_en: But Great Warhead, our packs are not here! We can't go to war like this!

t_ch: 狼群不在这里？不，我所行之处，皆为狼群！
t_en: Our packs are not here? Wherever I go, everyone is the pack!

t_ch: 末度，我们是位居食物链顶端的强者。
t_en: Mok Tok, we're the apex predators at the top of the hierarchy.

t_ch: 身为狼，我们是恐惧的制造者，而不是恐惧的奴隶。如果你无法看见道路，我将成为高悬的赤月，为你们照亮道路的所在——
t_en: As wolves, we create fear. We don't become servants to it. If you're blind to the path, I will be the crimson moon that lights the way for you.

t_ch: 和所有的弟兄们一起分享我的赤血。用它来感染狐人，用它来制造恐惧！
t_en: Share my crimson blood with our brethren! Use it to infect those foxians and strike fear into their hearts!

t_ch: 藏形匿影的猴子…给我出来！
t_en: Now, you devious monkey... Come out and face me!

t_ch: …椒丘。
t_en: ...Jiaoqiu.

t_ch: 貊泽…快走——
t_en: Moze... run...

t_ch: 他走不了，你也一样。曜青的猴子，你来的正是时候。告诉你们的将军……
t_en: No, he can't run, and neither can you. You've come at the right time, monkey of the Yaoqing. Tell your general...

t_ch: 告诉她，我将从这儿开始，杀穿罗浮仙舟。从现在开始，分享我狼血的子嗣会在每一处闹市中奔逐狩猎，以妖弓的信众为食。
t_en: Tell her, that I will unleash a massacre here, drowning the Xianzhou Luofu in blood. From this moment on, wolves bearing my blood will hunt on every street, feasting on the followers of that Devilish Archer.

t_ch: 狼子们，随我一同，在猎物之间高视阔步！
t_en: Follow me, my cubs! We shall stride among the prey!

t_ch: 赐狼以穷途，令其寻得前路。饲狼以死数，令其食能果腹。苟且偷安，无处得生；抵死鏖杀，万世长存……
t_en: Gift the wolf a dead end, where new paths arise. Raise him to a doomed fate, where satiation lies. The cowards in forgotten corners, meet their unworthy demise. Yet the valorous, in brutal battles, embrace their eternal prize...

t_ch: 听啊，这炮声隆隆——唤起了我心底所有关于战场的回忆……
t_en: Do you hear that? The rumble of the cannons... it brings back all the memories of past battles within me...

t_ch: 我的回归将带回「狼之古训」，我将以我自己的方式挽救堕落的族群，让它重焕荣光！
t_en: My return will bring back the Wolf's Creed. In my own way, I shall save our weakened pack and restore it to its former glory!

t_ch: 快撤！将消息带给末度大人！
t_en: Run! Tell Lord Mok Tok about this!

t_ch: 「演武仪典」即将召开\n\n竞锋舰
t_en: The Wardance is about to commence\n\nSkysplitter

t_ch: 竞锋舰
t_en: Skysplitter

t_ch: 呜哇，竞锋舰上的礼炮声好响啊！这就是「演武仪典」的气氛吗，感觉耳朵都要震聋了。
t_en: Wow! The Skysplitter's gun salute is really loud! So this is what the Wardance is actually like? I feel like my eardrums are going to burst.

t_ch: 场面不大，又怎么能吸引别人前来观看呢。三月，你没忘记自己是来做什么的吧？
t_en: Well, how could they expect to attract people to come and watch if they didn't make a huge spectacle? March, you haven't forgotten what you came to do, have you?

t_ch: 我…是来做什么的呢？
t_en: What... did I come to do?

t_ch: 我是谁？我在哪？我在这儿做什么？
t_en: Who am I? Where am I? What am I doing here?

t_ch: 看看周围，想起什么来了吗？
t_en: Look around you, do you remember anything?

t_ch: 啊，从天而降砸在泰科铵大球馆的回忆又涌了上来，我们…是来比赛的吧。
t_en: Ah, memories of falling from the sky into Taikiyan Stadium have come flooding back to me. We... came to compete, didn't we?

t_ch: 太好了太好了，你的脑袋终于恢复记忆了，我也放下了高高举起的「老铁」。
t_en: Great, you've finally gotten your memory back, and I can put down Old Mettle.

t_ch: 我代彦卿师父守擂！
t_en: I am here to be the ringmaster on behalf of Master Yanqing!

t_ch: 我代表罗浮仙舟，前来打败挑战者！
t_en: As a representative of the Xianzhou Luofu, I will defeat all challengers!

t_ch: 就是这股气势…很有精神嘛！三月，你要是能双腿打直了别发抖，那就看起来更有说服力了。
t_en: That's the spirit, sounds like you're all fired up! If you could just keep your legs from shaking so much, it would be more convincing, March.

t_ch: 我是来确保演武仪典安全的！
t_en: I am here to ensure the safety of the Wardance!

t_ch: 那是爷爷交托给我的任务，至于你，安心守住擂台就好。
t_en: That was the mission my grandfather entrusted to me. You just relax and keep guard over the ring.

t_ch: 俗话说得好，「有事弟子服其劳」。记住喽，你是以罗浮剑士的身份，代替你那个不肖的彦卿师父前来守擂的。
t_en: As the saying goes, "The disciple takes the toil of their master." Remember, you are here under the identity of a Luofu swordmaster, taking the place of your no-good Master Yanqing as the ringmaster.

t_ch: 说起来，要是这一回台上的对手超级厉害，三两下就把我打趴了，那可怎么办呀…毕竟我只算得上剑术初学者。也不知当时哪来的信心，就这么答应了怀炎将军。
t_en: But what am I supposed to do if my opponent for this round is super talented and just wipes the floor with me in seconds? I'm still a beginner in swordplay! I don't know where I got the confidence when I agreed to help General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 嗨呀，不用担心。你要是趴下了，就让为师来会会你的对手咯。只是这么一来，罗浮仙舟的光彩可就全都归朱明啦，哈哈哈哈哈……
t_en: Come on, there's no need to worry about that. If you are defeated, then your opponent shall have to answer to me! Although, if that happens, the honor and the glory of the Xianzhou Luofu shall all be taken by the Zhuming, hahaha...

t_ch: 拜托了，云璃师父，我都快要上场了，你就给我点信心吧！
t_en: Master Yunli, please, I'm about to compete! Can you say something that will give me a little confidence?

t_ch: 三月，想一想你们列车无名客一路旅行经历的大风大浪！你一定见识过比演武对手可怕的多的敌人吧？这么一想是不是轻松多了？
t_en: March. Think about the great storms that you Nameless of the Express have weathered along your many journeys. There's no doubt that you have faced far more terrifying enemies than your opponents here today, right? Don't you feel a lot more relaxed looking at it that way?

t_ch: 云璃师父，我还是退赛吧。
t_en: Master Yunli, I think I should drop out of the contest.

t_ch: 压力好大，我感觉自己肚子隐隐作痛，要不…我还是退赛吧？
t_en: There's so much pressure, my stomach is really hurting. Maybe... I should just drop out of the contest?

t_ch: 你答应了爷爷还想跑？快走！
t_en: You made a promise to my grandfather and now you want to run away? Get out there, now!

t_ch: 要不，还是把彦卿叫回来吧！
t_en: Why don't we call Yanqing back?

t_ch: 要不要把彦卿师父给叫回来？这当师父的急流勇退…让弟子垫上刀头，这叫哪门子事啊。
t_en: Why don't we call Master Yanqing back? What kind of master would retire at the height of their career and leave their disciple to fight in their place?

t_ch: 三月，师父退赛，这可是你出人头地的好机会啊。
t_en: March. Your master has dropped out of the contest. This is a great opportunity for you to stick your neck out!

t_ch: 我看是师父给我一个人头落地的好机会吧！
t_en: Stick my neck out and get my head chopped off, you mean!

t_ch: 哪儿来得这么多抱怨，你答应了爷爷还想跑？快走！
t_en: What is all of this complaining about? You made a promise to my grandfather and now you want to run away? Get out there, now!

t_ch: 咱们走吧。
t_en: Let's get going, then.

t_ch: 这些日子我苦练剑术，也该上场证明一下自己了。咱们走。
t_en: I've trained hard in the art of swordplay over these few days. It's time for me to get in the ring and prove myself. Let's go.

t_ch: 您好，请问有什么我可以帮到您吗？
t_en: Hello, is there anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 你好，我有些问题需要咨询！
t_en: Hello, I have some questions I'd like to ask you!

t_ch: …你还有问题要问吗？
t_en: ...You have questions?

t_ch: 请问您是受邀参加比赛的选手，还是观赛的观众呢？
t_en: May I ask, are you an invited contestant, or are you a spectator?

t_ch: 我是参赛选手！
t_en: I am a contestant!

t_ch: 那在此先预祝您宝剑常锋，登擂夺胜。
t_en: Then may your blade be sharp and your victory glorious!

t_ch: 我其实是看比赛的观众…
t_en: I'm just a spectator...

t_ch: 喂喂，还没上场就打退堂鼓了吗？你好，我们俩都是演武仪典的参赛选手。
t_en: Hey! Are you trying to back out before you even hit the arena? Hello, we are both Wardance competitors.

t_ch: 那就请沿着通道继续前进，会到达安检处，那里的工作人员会指引你们前往选手大厅的。
t_en: Then please continue along this passageway. You will come to a security checkpoint, and the staff there will guide you to the contestants' hall.

t_ch: 请沿着通道继续前进，会到达安检处，那里的工作人员会指引你们前往选手大厅的。
t_en: Please continue along this passageway. You will come to a security checkpoint, and the staff there will guide you to the contestants' hall.

t_ch: 这就是竞锋舰吗？虽然执勤的时候在地面上看还是挺气派的……
t_en: So this is the Skysplitter? It looked quite impressive from the ground where I was on duty, but...

t_ch: 但今天真的登舰了，感觉没有我想象中的…那么大？
t_en: But now that I'm on board, it just doesn't seem as... big as I imagined?

t_ch: 哦豁，你想象中的「气派的竞锋舰」是什么样子的呢？那么大？还是那么大？
t_en: Oh? And how big did you imagine this "impressive Skysplitter" to be? This big? This big, maybe?

t_ch: 我想想…嗯，要是从这里去往观众席，得搭星槎换乘，坐上两个时辰吧！不然不够气派啊！
t_en: Let me think... Hmm. I'd expect at least a four-hour journey by starskiff to the spectator seating from here. Otherwise it's simply not impressive enough!

t_ch: 那不就是单纯很大而已吗？
t_en: Wouldn't that be too big for no good reason?

t_ch: 大就是好，多就是美！我看这竞锋舰还是不够大，炮台也不够多！
t_en: The bigger, the better! If you ask me, the Skysplitter is nowhere near big enough and there are nowhere near enough cannons!

t_ch: 以前这艘舰船上的炮台还是蛮多的。
t_en: This warship used to have loads of cannons.

t_ch: 你在想一些不切实际的东西。
t_en: You're daydreaming about stuff that ain't gonna happen.

t_ch: 怎么了嘛，人还得是要有梦想的嘛！要我说啊，竞锋舰就是太小了！
t_en: What's wrong with that? People are supposed to dream big! If you ask me, the Skysplitter is far too small!

t_ch: 其实我也觉得这里有点小。
t_en: I kinda think this place is a bit cramped too.

t_ch: 远看这竞锋舰很小，登上一看，比想象的还要小。
t_en: The Skysplitter looks small from a distance, but it's even smaller than I imagined now that I'm on board.

t_ch: 你听听，这是我的同道中人。
t_en: Hear that? This person gets it!

t_ch: 主要是我想象中的竞锋舰是很大的，但是它和我想象的不太一样。
t_en: Either way, I thought that the Skysplitter would be absolutely huge, so it's not quite how I imagined it.

t_ch: 我说你到底走不走啊？
t_en: Are we going or not?

t_ch: 真的，这竞锋舰不够气派！
t_en: Seriously, the Skysplitter is nowhere near impressive enough!

t_ch: 听说这次演武仪典有一位从朱明仙舟来的剑士将要挑战彦卿骁卫，你觉得这两边打起来谁能赢？能不能理性分析一下？
t_en: I heard that a sword master from the Xianzhou Zhuming is going to challenge Lieutenant Yanqing at this Wardance. Who do you think will win in the fight? Can you give me your analysis?

t_ch: 那肯定是彦卿骁卫赢啊！他的剑术已臻化境，我有幸见过他在星槎海飞剑擒凶的英姿，三招两式就能……
t_en: Lieutenant Yanqing, obviously! His swordsmanship is on a whole other level! I was fortunate enough to see him in person using his Flying Swords to capture a criminal in Starskiff Haven. I reckon just a few swift strikes and...

t_ch: 咳，咳！
t_en: Ahem!

t_ch: 我的天，这不是来自朱明仙舟的选手云璃小姐吗！
t_en: Goodness! Isn't that the Xianzhou Zhuming's contestant, Miss Yunli?

t_ch: …三招两式就能被朱明的剑士打趴下！
t_en: ...Just a few swift strikes and the Zhuming swordmaster will have him on his knees!

t_ch: 看到我鼻子上架着的眼镜了吗？
t_en: You see these glasses I have on?

t_ch: 这是我从湛蓝星那边买来的特产「定分镜」，只要盯着选手瞧，就能给对方的战斗力定个评级。
t_en: These Rating Glasses are a special souvenir I picked up from The Blue. If I just look at a contestant through them, they can give a combat power rating.

t_ch: 朝我这儿看看？
t_en: Take a look at me?

t_ch: 那你看看我，我的战斗力是多少？
t_en: Take a look at me, then. What is my combat power rating?
...Five.

t_ch: 怎么可能，你再给我好好看看嘛！是不是满分也是5？
t_en: Impossible! Take a closer look! Is it scored out of five or something?

t_ch: 可怜的小三月，被机器的评分玩弄于股掌之间。别看了，快走吧！
t_en: Poor little March, being bullied by a mechanical scoring system... Don't pay them any mind, let's go!

t_ch: （快速走开）
t_en: (Leave quickly)

t_ch: 别别别，别往我这儿看。万一看到个打击人的数字也别说出来哈。
t_en: No-no-no! Don't look at me! If you see some embarrassingly low score, then I don't want to hear it!

t_ch: 我已收到驭空大人的知会，特来接应二位。
t_en: I was informed of your arrival by Madam Yukong and came to greet you personally.

t_ch: 说起来，三月小姐要代彦卿骁卫守擂的事已经公示了。我刚才从选手大厅经过的时候，听见选手们正在议论纷纷，其中最多的问题就是——「三月七是谁？」
t_en: The news that you will be the ringmaster in place of Lieutenant Yanqing has been made public, Miss March. Just now, as I passed the contestants' hall, I overheard them all discussing this news. The most common question I heard was... "Who is March 7th?"

t_ch: 他们要是问起，三月小姐该如何自我介绍，您准备好了吗？
t_en: If they ask you this, how will you introduce yourself? Do you have something prepared?

t_ch: 三月七，星穹列车的美少女。
t_en: March 7th, young beauty of the Astral Express.

t_ch: 三月七，一位来自星穹列车的美少女。
t_en: March 7th, a young beauty from the Astral Express!

t_ch: 好啊，在别人问你的时候你不妨就这么大声说出来。
t_en: Alright! When people ask you, you might as well tell them this loud and clear.

t_ch: 三月七，彦卿的弟子。
t_en: March 7th, Yanqing's disciple.

t_ch: 我是彦卿骁卫座下弟子…这么一想也算是景元将军的徒孙。如此说来，我来头还挺不小的！
t_en: I am a disciple of Lieutenant Yanqing... That means that I'm also technically a grand-disciple of General Jing Yuan. When you put it like that, I'm actually a pretty big deal!

t_ch: 可恶，竟然把为师的名头抛到了一边…气死我了。
t_en: Ugh! You completely left my name out... How rude!

t_ch: 三月七，无敌的三月七！
t_en: March 7th, the invincible March 7th!

t_ch: 三月七，无敌的三月七！
t_en: March 7th, the invincible March 7th!

t_ch: 至少下一次虚张声势的时候，底气一定要足。
t_en: Next time you try to bluff like that, at least make it convincing.

t_ch: 失败者不配问我的名字。
t_en: Losers have no right to ask my name.

t_ch: 哼，失败者不配知道本姑娘的名字。
t_en: Hmph. Losers have no right to know my great name!

t_ch: 这是什么反派登场台词吗？
t_en: Is this some kind of villain entrance line?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，看来二位还是挺轻松的，临危受命能有这样的状态可不容易。
t_en: Hahaha, I see you are both quite relaxed! Impressive, given the imminent danger you face!

t_ch: 演武仪典这边早就登记过云璃小姐的选手信息了。三月七小姐则是临时入选这场比赛，天舶司已经为你准备好相关的手续了。
t_en: Miss Yunli's contestant information was registered for the Wardance a long time ago. However, as a last-minute entry, the Sky-Faring Commission has taken care of the necessary procedures for you, Miss March 7th.

t_ch: 两位请先沿着这条路上去，选手大厅就在前方。
t_en: Please follow this path. The contestant hall is just up ahead.

t_ch: 谢谢你，夕葵姐姐。
t_en: Thank you, Miss Xikui.

t_ch: 看来这就是选手大厅了。
t_en: So, this is the contestant hall.

t_ch: 好多人啊。
t_en: There are so many people!

t_ch: 三月，在「演武仪典」开始前，让为师再给你上一课。
t_en: March, before the Wardance begins, allow me to give you one more lesson.

t_ch: 你知道吗，爷爷常对我说，物肖主人形。一个人的武器会反映出他的习惯和性格。
t_en: My grandfather always told me that "a weapon mimics its master." That means that a person's weapon will reflect their habits and personality.

t_ch: 你见过我挥动我那把{RUBY_B#大剑}「老铁」{RUBY_E#}的样子吧？说说看，你观察到什么了？
t_en: You've seen me wield {RUBY_B#greatsword}Old Mettle{RUBY_E#} before, haven't you? Tell me, what did you observe from that?

t_ch: 云璃师父，力气很大。
t_en: That Master Yunli is very strong.

t_ch: 云璃师父的「老铁」看着比人都高，能挥动这样的武器，显然应该有一身怪力吧？
t_en: Master Yunli's Old Mettle is taller than a person. Being able to wield a weapon like that must mean that you have ridiculous strength, right?

t_ch: 云璃师父，性格很暴力。
t_en: Master Yunli has a fiery temperament.

t_ch: 挥舞这么巨大的武器…云璃师傅比起策略更喜欢压倒性的暴力呢。
t_en: Waving around such a giant weapon... Master Yunli favors using overwhelming force over strategy.

t_ch: 你说的不错。我的战术向来追求「简单有效」，砍就完事了。
t_en: You are not wrong. My battle style has always been "simple and effective" — one cut and it's over.

t_ch: 云璃师父，审美水平有待提高。
t_en: Master Yunli doesn't have good taste.

t_ch: 云璃师傅的剑看起来又粗又重，审美水平有待提高啊。
t_en: Master Yunli's weapon looks so thick and heavy. I don't think you have very good taste.

t_ch: 我让你猜对手的战术风格，谁让你评价我的「老铁」了。
t_en: I asked you to make predictions about your opponent's combat style. No one asked you to criticize my dear Old Mettle!

t_ch: 观察对手使用的武器，判断他们擅长使用什么样的战术，又有哪些短板，这就是争取胜利的捷径。比如重量惊人的巨剑，显然不利久战，所以要选择拖延时间的战术。
t_en: Observing the weapon that your opponent uses, assessing what kind of battle skills they excel at and where their weaknesses lie is the key to victory in battle. A shockingly heavy sword like mine, for example, is obviously not suited for a long, drawn-out battle. So, it would be best to find a way to drag the battle out.

t_ch: 你怎么把自己的弱点告诉我了？
t_en: Why would you tell me your weakness?

t_ch: 这样才算是相对的公平较量嘛，毕竟为师也不想看你输在别人手里。
t_en: That's the only way to ensure that we have a fair fight, don't you think? I don't want to see you go and lose to someone else, after all.

t_ch: 眼下是个好机会，就趁现在试着学学如何评估你的对手吧，就用这个大厅里的人练练手。知己知彼，才能百战不殆。
t_en: Now is a perfect opportunity to learn how to evaluate your opponents. Let's use the people here in the contestants' hall to practice. If you know yourself and know your enemy, you will not lose in a hundred battles.

t_ch: 知己知彼…让我看看，现在问问谁好呢？
t_en: Know myself and know the enemy... Let's see, who should I ask first?

t_ch: 可恶，久闻彦卿骁卫的大名，来这儿也就是想见见他，和他切磋一下。
t_en: Argh! I came here because I wanted to meet the great Lieutenant Yanqing I've heard so much about and finally cross blades with him myself!

t_ch: 谁能想到他竟然收了徒弟，还让自己的徒弟代替他守擂。这在我们曜青可是不战而逃的奇耻大辱啊！
t_en: Who would have imagined that he would take a disciple and have her be the ringmaster in his place? This is no more than running away from a fight and is a great dishonor to the Yaoqing!

t_ch: 这个临时顶替他的三月七究竟是谁啊？
t_en: This last-minute replacement, March 7th, who exactly is she?

t_ch: （仔细观察对方的武器）
t_en: (Carefully observe their weapon)

t_ch: 这位来自曜青的选手除去背后的长剑外，竟还随身携带了一柄电磁手弩。
t_en: The contestant from the Yaoqing, aside from the longsword on the back, also carried an electromagnetic hand crossbow.

t_ch: （早、早知道能同时使用多种武器，我就带上自己的弓了。）
t_en: (If... if I'd known I could use multiple weapons at once, I'd have brought my bow.)

t_ch: 小姑娘，你好啊。你也是来参加守擂竞锋的吗？
t_en: Hello, Miss. Are you here to take part in the Ringmaster's Challenge, too?

t_ch: 你好，我就是三月七。
t_en: Hello, I am March 7th.

t_ch: 没错，我就是那个三月七。
t_en: That's right. I'm the March 7th that everyone's been talking about.

t_ch: 什么？！你就是三月七！！
t_en: What!? You're March 7th!?

t_ch: 放心吧，我只是个练习剑术时长半个月的剑士。
t_en: Don't worry, I'm a newbie swordmaster who's only been practicing the art of swordplay for half a month.

t_ch: 三月七，站在你面前的是…习练剑法超过两百年的云骑老战士！
t_en: March 7th... Standing before you is a seasoned Cloud Knight who has practiced swordsmanship for over two hundred years!

t_ch: （扮猪吃老虎，让对方轻敌也算是一种战术吧？）
t_en: (Tricking the opponent into underestimating me is a valid tactic too, right?)

t_ch: 只是个练剑不久的小丫头…为何她也会被选上？
t_en: She's just a little girl that has only just started training... Why was she chosen as a contestant?

t_ch: …今天穿的这身衣服还真是有些不习惯啊。
t_en: ...I'm not used to wearing clothes like this at all...

t_ch: 对了，你就是代替彦卿骁卫登场的三月七选手！没错吧？
t_en: Oh! You're March 7th, the stand-in for Lieutenant Yanqing, correct?

t_ch: 没错。您是…？
t_en: That's right, and you are...?

t_ch: 如您所见，我是负责现场赛事采访的记者，没错。
t_en: As you can see, I am the journalist entrusted with interviewing contestants on-site.

t_ch: 天舶司作为主办方准备的还挺周全，有点意思。
t_en: I see the Sky-Faring Commission has really gone all out as the host. Interesting...

t_ch: 是啊，像这样的大场合里，一般少不了咱们这样的人。对了，趁着比赛开始，我能问些自己感兴趣的问题吗？
t_en: That's right! A big event like this just can't go ahead without a few of us running around. Since the competition is getting started, could I ask you some questions that are on my mind?

t_ch: 当然可以。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 太、太好了！
t_en: Oh, great!

t_ch: 其实我是来知己知彼的……
t_en: I'm actually just here to learn about my opponents...

t_ch: 知己知彼，其中就包括了解自己对吧？接受采访就能很好了解自己。
t_en: Know yourself and know your enemy, right? If you accept my interview, you'll learn all about yourself!

t_ch: 首先是大家最关心的问题！彦卿骁卫没有出现在今天的「演武仪典」上。
t_en: First, the question that is on everyone's minds! Lieutenant Yanqing is not appearing in today's Wardance!

t_ch: 根据一些不可靠的小道消息，我听说彦卿骁卫现在身负重伤，请问这是真的吗？
t_en: This is just gossip from unreliable sources, but I hear that Lieutenant Yanqing is seriously injured. Is this true?

t_ch: 是真的！
t_en: It's true!

t_ch: 竟然是这样，请问他是为什么身负重伤呢？
t_en: If that's the case, could you please tell me how these serious injuries came about?

t_ch: 交通事故。
t_en: A traffic accident.

t_ch: 难道是…星槎的交通事故？真是危险！
t_en: You mean... a starskiff crash!? Why, how terrible!

t_ch: 练习受伤。
t_en: He was injured in training.

t_ch: 剑术练习还真是不容易呢。
t_en: Swordplay is a truly difficult art!

t_ch: 遭人暗算。
t_en: There was a plot against him.

t_ch: 竟、竟然有这种事！这可是大新闻！噗，对不起，虽然不太礼貌，但…该不会是三月小姐干的吧。
t_en: I—I had no idea it was that serious! That's a huge scoop! *ahem* I do not wish to offend you with this question but... This was not your doing, was it, Miss March?

t_ch: 讨厌，真是敏锐的直觉啊。要不然我怎么会站在这儿呢。
t_en: Hmph, you saw right through me. Why else would I be standing here today?

t_ch: 让徒弟出人头地。
t_en: He wanted his disciple to have this opportunity.

t_ch: 等等，师父为了让徒弟出人头地而身受重伤退赛？哦哦，如此爱惜弟子的师父可不多见啊。
t_en: Wait. Your master got himself seriously injured so he could withdraw from the competition and allow you to compete in his place? Wow, not many masters would show such devotion to their disciples.

t_ch: 是假的。
t_en: It's not true.

t_ch: 那真实的原因是？
t_en: Then what is the real reason?

t_ch: 消极比赛。
t_en: He just doesn't want to compete.

t_ch: 没想到备受关注的天才少年也会有消极比赛的情况。
t_en: I never thought that such a talented young man that has garnered so much attention would have this kind of attitude!

t_ch: 无可奉告。
t_en: No comment.

t_ch: 怎么会这样？好吧，我也是能理解的。
t_en: Seriously? Ah, alright, I understand...

t_ch: 感情问题。
t_en: Relationship trouble.

t_ch: 竟、竟然有这种事！这可是大新闻！
t_en: Y—You're kidding!? That's a huge scoop!

t_ch: 你自己都说是小道消息了…
t_en: You yourself said that this was just gossip...

t_ch: 噗，对不起，我实在表现得不太专业。看来彦卿骁卫也有自己的任务在身啊。那就让我们期待三月小姐在赛场上一展英姿吧。
t_en: Ah, my apologies, this was very unprofessional of me. I see that Lieutenant Yanqing has his own affairs to tend to. Then let us look forward to seeing a valiant performance from you, Miss March!

t_ch: 演武仪典开始后，三月七小姐将会接受来自不同世界的剑术高手挑战，这其中是否有你比较期待的对手呢？
t_en: Once the Wardance begins, you will be challenged by master swordsmen from many different worlds, Miss March 7th. Are there any that you are looking forward to facing in particular?

t_ch: 有的。
t_en: There are.

t_ch: 请问是哪位选手呢？
t_en: May I ask who that might be?

t_ch: 彦卿师父。
t_en: Master Yanqing.

t_ch: 哦哦哦！果然还得是万众瞩目的彦卿骁卫！
t_en: Ohhh! Of course it's the highly-anticipated Lieutenant Yanqing!

t_ch: 云璃师父。
t_en: Master Yunli.

t_ch: 来自朱明仙舟的天才剑术少女云璃。很期待两位交手的盛况！
t_en: The talented swordswoman from the Xianzhou Zhuming, Miss Yunli! I look forward to a spectacular battle between you two!

t_ch: 飞霄将军。
t_en: General Feixiao.

t_ch: 三月七选手无视了在场所有人，扬言要挑战曜青仙舟的「天击将军」。
t_en: So March 7th is ignoring everyone around her and is declaring her will to challenge the Merlin's Claw of the Xianzhou Yaoqing.

t_ch: 素裳。
t_en: Sushang.

t_ch: 素、素裳…那是谁？最近好像经常在网上看到过这个名字。
t_en: S—Sushang...? Who is that? I feel like that's a name I've seen a lot online recently...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}。
t_en: {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 呃，谁？请问…您说的这位是参赛选手吗？
t_en: Uhh, who? Are you... talking about a contestant here today?

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 三月七选手真是太狂…呃不，太自信了！
t_en: March 7th, you are quite arro—... I mean, extremely self-assured!

t_ch: 很感谢你接受采访，谢谢！
t_en: Thank you very much for accepting my interview. Much appreciated!

t_ch: 很感谢你接受采访，谢谢！
t_en: Thank you very much for accepting my interview. Much appreciated!

t_ch: 找我？嗯，你有什么想问的吗？
t_en: Uh... Was there something you wanted to ask me?

t_ch: （仔细观察对方的…佩剑）
t_en: (Carefully observe their... sword)

t_ch: 嗯…这只是普通的云骑阵刀。
t_en: Ah, yes... This is just a regular Cloud Knight devastator glaive.

t_ch: 你有什么弱点吗？
t_en: Do you have any weaknesses?

t_ch: 非要问我的弱点的话…我一开口就会导致冷场，这算是弱点吗？
t_en: If I had to think of a weakness... Whenever I open my mouth, I make things awkward. Does that count as a weakness?

t_ch: 我明白了，三月七小姐、云璃小姐，我不是参赛选手，我是负责赛事安保的卫兵。
t_en: I understand what's going on, Miss March 7th and Miss Yunli. I'm actually not a contestant. I'm just here as a security guard.

t_ch: 那…刚才是有些尴尬哈。
t_en: Ah, so... that was a little bit awkward.

t_ch: 没事，我也经常不忍心看别人冷场而接话，结果导致尴尬的人变成自己。我已经习惯尴尬了，请不必在意。
t_en: It's alright. I can't bear to watch other people make things awkward, so I always try to keep the conversation going, but then I'm the one who ends up making things awkward. I've gotten used to it by now, so don't worry.

t_ch: 有发现什么状况吗？
t_en: Have you noticed anything out of the ordinary?

t_ch: 没什么状况，可以说是风平「狼」静。
t_en: Nothing out of the ordinary. I guess you could say it's all fair winds and calm "wolves."

t_ch: 果然又冷场了…你们理解笑点了吗？「浪」和「狼」同音，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: Another awkward pause, I knew it... Do you get it? "Wolves" sounds like "waves." It's a funny pun.

t_ch: 这个人竟然解释笑话！
t_en: He's actually explaining his joke!

t_ch: 唉，开口就冷场，果然是我的弱点啊。
t_en: Ah, making things awkward really is my weakness...

t_ch: 来客还在陆续增加，请千万小心。
t_en: There are still many guests arriving here. Please be cautious!

t_ch: 明白！
t_en: Understood!

t_ch: 我不是参赛选手，三月小姐请找别人聊天吧。
t_en: I am not a contestant Miss March. Please find someone else to talk to.

t_ch: 「演武仪典」的第一场赛事，就快开始了。三月你还好吧？
t_en: The first round of the Wardance is about to begin. Are you feeling okay, March?

t_ch: 我？我还好。一看到这么多人聚在一块，不知怎么突然安心了起来。
t_en: Me? I'm fine. As soon as I saw all these people gathered here, I suddenly felt weirdly relaxed.

t_ch: 选手们全都很可靠。
t_en: These contestants all seem very reliable.

t_ch: 看着这些选手，我不知为何突然心不慌了。
t_en: Looking at these contestants, I suddenly feel a lot calmer for some reason...

t_ch: 好像…没有什么特别的感受？
t_en: I don't... really feel anything special?

t_ch: 你登台交手的时候，维持这样的平常心就好。
t_en: Just make sure to maintain this composure when you go in the ring.

t_ch: 我明白云璃师父让我「知己知彼」的用意了，没有刚才那么紧张了！
t_en: I understand why you wanted me to "know myself and know my enemy." I don't feel as nervous as I did before!

t_ch: …怎么说，要去看看擂场了吗？
t_en: ...So, do you want to go out and see the ring?

t_ch: 我打算继续知己知彼！
t_en: I want to keep learning about my opponents!

t_ch: …也行，你去问吧。
t_en: ...Very well, go and get your intel.

t_ch: 想去看看赛场的样子！
t_en: I want to see what the ring looks like!

t_ch: 我就知道，走吧。
t_en: I knew it. Let's go.

t_ch: 汪汪，汪汪汪汪？
t_en: Woof woof, woof woof woof woof?

t_ch: 欸…连谛听也要问？
t_en: Uhh... You're even asking Diting?

t_ch: （尝试翻译一下谛听说了什么）
t_en: (Try to translate what Diting is saying...)

t_ch: 汪汪汪汪汪汪汪。
t_en: Woof woof woof woof, woof woof woof.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，「我不是参赛选手哦」，我明白啦。
t_en: Ah, it's saying "I'm not a contestant." Got it.

t_ch: 汪汪，汪汪汪汪汪？
t_en: Woof woof, woof woof woof woof woof?

t_ch: 汪汪。
t_en: Woof woof.

t_ch: 看起来是问完了……
t_en: Looks like she's done with her questions...

t_ch: 你就别再继续知己知彼了，走吧，我想去看看擂场。
t_en: That's enough knowing the enemy for now. Come on, I want to see what the ring looks like.

t_ch: 或许是长时间和{NICKNAME}相处，让三月七产生了「可以问问长椅」的冲动。
t_en: Maybe it's because she spends so much time with {NICKNAME} that March 7th has the urge to interrogate the bench.

t_ch: 但是三月七并不是{NICKNAME}，三月七的精神状况也没有…那么糟糕。
t_en: But March 7th isn't {NICKNAME}, and her mental condition isn't... that bad.

t_ch: 嗯…有什么想问的，可以抓紧问，不然「三月七的理智」就要回来了。
t_en: Hmm...If there's anything you want to ask, hurry up or March 7th's rationality will come back.

t_ch: 现场有厉害的选手来过吗？
t_en: Did any skilled competitors show up?

t_ch: 长椅沉默不语，长椅见过太多厉害的人，其中就包括曾经的罗浮剑首。
t_en: The bench remains silent, having witnessed the presence of many a formidable figure, including the once-renowned Sword Champion of the Luofu.

t_ch: （云璃师父正在看着，冷静！）
t_en: (Master Yunli is watching. Calm down!)

t_ch: 三月七努力收起和长椅聊天的冲动，冲云璃点了点头。
t_en: March 7th tries very hard to contain her urge to talk to the bench and nods at Yunli.

t_ch: 长椅看着三月七的背影，流下了一滴汗。
t_en: The bench gazes at March 7th's back as a single bead of sweat drops from it.

t_ch: 你就别再继续知己知彼了，走吧，我想去看看擂场。
t_en: That's enough knowing the enemy for now. Come on, I want to see what the ring looks like.

t_ch: 先别着急去擂场，在这里了解一下你的对手吧。
t_en: There's no need to rush to the ring just yet, let's get to know your opponents around here first.

t_ch: 哇哦——！这就是从观众席望过去的感觉……
t_en: Wow! So this is what it's like from the spectator seats?

t_ch: 你看那边，那边就是我们要登上的擂台。
t_en: Look over there, that's the ring we'll be standing on.

t_ch: 云璃师父，我、我怎么好像又紧张起来了！我真的能做到吗？
t_en: Master Yunli, I—I'm getting nervous again! Can I really do this?

t_ch: 已经来不及让你紧张了，时间就要到了！
t_en: It's too late for nerves now. It's almost time!

t_ch: 什么时间就要到了。
t_en: Almost time for what?

t_ch: 一想到怀炎将军交代的使命，我的心就一直扑扑直跳，云璃师父，怎么办啊？
t_en: As soon as I think about the mission General Huaiyan gave me, my heart starts racing like crazy! What do I do, Master Yunli?

t_ch: 你的心要是不跳了那才麻烦。好好感受一下气氛，做好准备吧。
t_en: It's when it stops beating that we need to worry. Just take in the atmosphere and prepare yourself.

t_ch: 我得暂时离开，去舰上各处好好检查检查，这也是爷爷交代的使命呢。
t_en: I have to leave for a moment and do some inspections around the ship. This was also part of grandpa's orders.

t_ch: 唉，也不知道彦卿师父那边怎么样了……
t_en: I wonder how Master Yanqing is doing over there...

t_ch: 你给我专心一点。
t_en: Hey, focus!

t_ch: 云璃师父，我感觉我还没有准备充分……
t_en: Master Yunli, I don't think I'm completely ready...

t_ch: 三月，准备永远是不充分的。
t_en: March, there is no such thing as completely ready.

t_ch: 演武仪典就要开始了…
t_en: The Wardance is about to begin...

t_ch: 咱们也算是托工作的福…
t_en: I guess we got pretty lucky with this job...

t_ch: 听说这次来了不少高手。
t_en: I hear there's quite a few masters here...

t_ch: 听说彦卿骁卫不会登场…
t_en: I hear Lieutenant Yanqing isn't competing anymore...

t_ch: 走，先进场吧！
t_en: Let's go, it's time to enter the stadium!

t_ch: 云璃小姐，三月小姐！两位这边走！
t_en: Miss Yunli, Miss March! This way, please.

t_ch: 走，让我们去选手大厅转转。
t_en: Come, let's take a look around the contestant hall.

t_ch: 从这边走能够看到演武仪典的正式会场。
t_en: If we walk through here, we'll be able to see the Wardance's official venue.

t_ch: 报告将军，队伍已各就其位。天舶司对星槎航道的管制也全部完成。
t_en: General, report! The teams are in position, and the Sky-Faring Commission has taken control of the starskiff lanes.

t_ch: 让所有机巧鸟动起来，扫描每个打算出入港口的人。
t_en: Activate all cycranes and have them scan everyone coming in and out of the port.

t_ch: 时刻戒备，一旦有可疑的异动，要在最短的时间内对其进行压制，避免事态扩散。
t_en: Remain vigilant at all times. Any suspicious movements must be suppressed as quickly as possible before the situation gets out of hand.

t_ch: 是！
t_en: Yes, general!

t_ch: 彦卿，{NICKNAME}，随我来。
t_en: Yanqing, {NICKNAME}, follow me.

t_ch: 都给我退开……
t_en: All of you, get out of my way...

t_ch: 是貊泽…
t_en: It's Moze...

t_ch: 将军，看那儿…
t_en: General, look there...

t_ch: 等等，别靠近。
t_en: Wait, don't go any closer.

t_ch: 将军…我失手了。那条疯狗把我留在了这儿，是要向你……
t_en: General... I failed you. That mad dog left me here as...

t_ch: 宣战。
t_en: A declaration of war.

t_ch: 是啊，我可是一直强忍着撕开他喉咙的冲动。毕竟，呼雷大人命我们留在此处，就是想看看……
t_en: That's right. I have been holding back the urge to slit his throat all this time. Well, Lord Hoolay ordered us to remain here, just to see...

t_ch: 你这位大名鼎鼎的天击将军，步离人的死敌，有没有能耐陪他一同进行…这场狩猎游戏？
t_en: ...if you, the great Merlin's Claw, mortal enemy of the borisin, are able to join him in a little... hunting game...

t_ch: 狩猎可不是什么游戏。它是一场你死我活的战争，准备好受死了吧？孽物！
t_en: Hunting is not a game, it is a battle of life and death. Are you ready for your death, abomination!?

t_ch: 貊泽，你没事吧？
t_en: Moze, are you okay?

t_ch: 我…没能救回椒丘。呼雷比我想像得更狡诈，也更难对付。
t_en: I... wasn't able to save Jiaoqiu. Hoolay was more cunning and powerful than I imagined...

t_ch: 说说看，他打算怎么向我宣战…是单枪匹马的决斗？还是交换人质？
t_en: Tell me, how is he planning to declare this war against me...? With a one-on-one duel? Or with a hostage exchange, maybe?

t_ch: 呼雷计划袭击罗浮仙舟上的闹市。
t_en: Hoolay is planning on attacking the bustling streets of the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 你说什么？
t_en: What did you just say?

t_ch: 这怎么可能？就算还有潜伏的步离人伪装者没能揪出来，呼雷这一行也不过数十人，他打算同时袭击罗浮仙舟的闹市？
t_en: Impossible! Even if there are still undercover borisin that we haven't sniffed out, Hoolay can't have more than a few dozen people. How is he planning on simultaneously attacking the streets of the Xianzhou Luofu?

t_ch: 这才是那家伙的底牌。呼雷的体内潜藏着步离人之间世代传承的寿瘟祸迹。我亲眼看见他将一个狐人转变成了步离狼卒……
t_en: He has an ace up his sleeve. Hoolay's body holds a plaguemark passed down through generations of borisin. I've seen him convert a foxian to a borisin wolftrooper with my own eyes...

t_ch: 这就是呼雷的「宣战」。他的血…能让狐人迅速扭曲变形、陷入疯狂，他将自己的血交给了手下，想在罗浮各处散布，掀起恐慌！
t_en: This is Hoolay's "declaration of war." His blood... It can cause foxians to rapidly transform and lose their minds. He has passed his blood to his minions and is planning to spread it across the Luofu and stir up panic!

t_ch: 也就是说到处都会发生袭击？
t_en: You're saying that there will be attacks all over?

t_ch: 想以一己之力「包围」我们…还真是个出人意料的怪物。
t_en: He wants to "encircle" us in one fell swoop... This beast is truly unpredictable.

t_ch: 赶快疏散所有狐人吧！
t_en: Evacuate all the foxians immediately!

t_ch: 立刻联系天舶司和云骑，启动通行禁令，要求所有的狐人闭门不出。
t_en: Contact the Sky-Faring Commission and the Cloud Knights right away! Have them enforce a traffic ban and order all foxians to remain indoors until further notice!

t_ch: 在看到敌人下战书的那一刻，就意味着对方已经开始行动了。
t_en: The moment you see your enemy's declaration of war, it means that they have already begun to take action.

t_ch: 可是…一旦呼雷制造的恐慌蔓延开来——
t_en: But... if Hoolay succeeds in getting this panic to spread—

t_ch: 还记得我告诉你们的吗？狩猎，应当考虑猎物的所想，而不是一味追逐猎物的踪迹。
t_en: Do you remember what I told you before? Hunting is about thinking in the same way as your prey, not just mindlessly chasing after them.

t_ch: 呼雷想要的就是罗浮上所有人陷入恐慌，他想看我们自乱阵脚，用有限的人力在罗浮每一寸土地上捕风捉影，寻找袭击到来的征兆，最后筋疲力尽。
t_en: What Hoolay wants is to see everyone on the Luofu descend into panic. He wants to see us lose our minds and waste our limited manpower scouring every inch of the Luofu for signs of an attack until we exhaust ourselves.

t_ch: 就像曜青人宴饮时爱玩的游戏：用几个碗盖住一样物什，移来换去，最后要你猜猜东西藏在哪个碗底下。
t_en: It's like that party game that the people of the Yaoqing love to play: They hide an object under one of several bowls, then move the bowls around, and have you guess which one the object is underneath.

t_ch: 但在我看来，这些都不过是障眼法——
t_en: The way I see it, this is all nothing more than misdirection...

t_ch: 无论呼雷做什么，都没法改变一个事实：他被困在了罗浮仙舟上。想要逃离此处，唯一的办法就是寻到船只。
t_en: No matter what Hoolay does, there is one truth that cannot be changed: He is trapped aboard the Xianzhou Luofu. The only way for him to escape is to find a ship.

t_ch: 他唯一能看到的船…
t_en: And the only ship he is able to see is...

t_ch: 只剩下天空中的竞锋舰了？
t_en: So his only option is the Skysplitter?

t_ch: 眼下…他唯一能看到的船，只有天空中的竞锋舰了。
t_en: Right now... the only ship he'd be able to see is the Skysplitter.

t_ch: 对他而言，那上面有数不清的人质。那里会是最理想的去处，也是我们最后的战场。
t_en: In his eyes, that ship is full of countless hostages. That would be the best place for him to go. So that will also be our final battlefield.

t_ch: 可如果我们猜错了…他去了别处，又或者就像他所说的——他的爪牙会带上狼血在各处感染狐人掀起混乱…我们又该怎么办？
t_en: But, if we're wrong about this... If he goes somewhere else, or if he does as he said he would, and his minions infect the foxians everywhere with wolf blood... What do we do then?

t_ch: 难道我们要放弃那些「可能性更小」的地方吗？
t_en: Are we just supposed to abandon those "less likely" locations?

t_ch: 这就是你在剑术之外要学的另一课：「权衡」和「取舍」，我们永远在扪心自问却又永远无解的问题。
t_en: This is another lesson that you must learn outside of swordplay: Weighing your options and making a decision. There are always questions that we will ask ourselves but never be able to answer.

t_ch: 在做出决定前，我们要杀死内心的犹豫；在做出决定后，我们要和悔恨缠斗。
t_en: Before making a decision, we must destroy any hesitation that we are holding onto. Once we make a decision, we must fight off any regrets.

t_ch: 我们可能会选错方向，令无辜的战友白白牺牲；我们可能猜对了敌人的动向，却错估了对手的实力，折戟败北……
t_en: There is a chance that we will make the wrong decision and allow our allies to be sacrificed in vain. There is a chance that we will predict our enemy's movements correctly, yet underestimate their strength and be overwhelmed in battle...

t_ch: 但犹豫不前比犯错更加致命。无论如何，我们都要作出决定。
t_en: But being lost in hesitation and failing to take any action will always be more harmful than taking the wrong path. We must make a decision, no matter what.

t_ch: 各位，听我命令。我会坐镇星槎海，疏散人群，应对所有可能到来的袭击。我会倾全力迎战，确保地面的安全。
t_en: Everyone, listen up. I will personally take charge of Starskiff Haven, evacuate the people, and prepare for any possible attacks. I will do my best to ensure that things are safe on the ground.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，请你像建木重生时那样，助仙舟一臂之力。我要拜托你和貊泽在这儿搜寻椒丘的下落。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, please lend us your strength as you did when the Ambrosial Arbor was reborn. I need you and Moze to go in search of Jiaoqiu.

t_ch: 交给我吧！
t_en: Leave it to me!

t_ch: 将军，请将竞锋舰的安全交给我！我本该出现在擂台上，现在我也必须重新回到竞锋舰上去。
t_en: General, please entrust me with the safety of the Skysplitter! I was originally meant to participate in the tournament, so now it is only right that I return to the Skysplitter.

t_ch: 如果呼雷袭击了竞锋舰…彦卿骁卫，请你务必尽力困住他，直到我赶来为止。
t_en: If Hoolay does come to attack the Skysplitter... Lieutenant Yanqing, I need you to hold him there until I am able to arrive.

t_ch: 将军应当对我有信心。若他敢登上竞锋舰，彦卿以手中之剑起誓，绝不让他逃离！
t_en: Please rest assured, General. If he dares step foot on the Skysplitter, I, Yanqing, swear on the blade in my hands that he shall not escape!

t_ch: 随着云骑开赴各处，封锁与排查迅速展开。\n\n此时，在长乐天，云骑正列阵待命……
t_en: As the Cloud Knights set off to various locations, they swiftly began to cordon off areas and start inspections.\n\n At that time, the Cloud Knights are waiting in formation in the Exalting Sanctum...

t_ch: 诸位！在仙舟的疆域上，步离人竟然狂妄地向我们发起挑战，放言要血洗仙舟的闹市街巷！
t_en: Cloud Knights! The borisin have arrogantly decided to challenge us on Xianzhou territory! They vow that they will unleash a bloodbath along the streets of the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 我们的身后，是手无寸铁的仙舟百姓和迢迢而来的旅客。试问，身为云骑军人，我们能让步离人的计划得逞吗？！
t_en: The unarmed civilians of the Xianzhou and countless visitors from afar are under our protection. Let me ask you, as Cloud Knights, will we allow the borisin to succeed with their plan!?

t_ch: 绝不允许！
t_en: Never!

t_ch: 随时戒备！
t_en: Be on your guard!

t_ch: 呼叫星槎海，所有云骑全面戒备，防范步离人的袭击！
t_en: Calling Starskiff Haven. Have all Cloud Knights be on full alert for borisin attacks!

t_ch: 飞霄，星槎海各处巷道中都有狼形生物在高速移动——
t_en: Feixiao, the ports of Starskiff Haven are crawling with wolf-like creatures. They're moving fast—

t_ch: 撑住，我马上就到！
t_en: Hold them back, I will be there immediately!

t_ch: 貊泽的信号就在附近。但是在一刻之前，他断开了联系……
t_en: Moze's signal is nearby, but we lost contact just a moment ago...

t_ch: 莫非……
t_en: Could that mean...

t_ch: 他会没事的。云骑，搜索目标！
t_en: He will be fine. Cloud Knights, search for the target!

t_ch: 贱畜，受死！受死！
t_en: Die, you lowly beast! Die!

t_ch: 省省力气，给我收声！
t_en: Save your breath!

t_ch: 别得意…
t_en: Don't think you've won...

t_ch: 战首为你们准备了…一条死路…
t_en: The Warhead has prepared... a road of no return for all of you...

t_ch: 我也是个像模像样的选手…
t_en: I'm a decent contestant too...

t_ch: 你？得再打起点精神！
t_en: You? You need to perk up more!
